name: app-layout
description: Server-side HTML render function factory with plugin architecture for injecting scripts, styles, theme attributes, analytics, and microfrontend version info into SSR page shells.
when_to_use: Server-side HTML render function factory with plugin architecture for injecting scripts, styles, theme attributes, analytics, and microfrontend version info into SSR page shells
avoid: Not for client-side rendering — server-side (Node.js) only. Not for streaming-agnostic setups without the plugin API.
not_for: Not for client-side rendering — server-side (Node.js) only. Not for streaming-agnostic setups without the plugin API.

name: createRenderFunction
description: Creates a server-side HTML render function for Gravity UI applications. Accepts an optional list of plugins to extend head and body content. The returned function accepts RenderParams and produces a complete HTML string.
when_to_use: Rendering a full HTML page on the server (Express/Koa/Fastify)
Adding analytics, theme attributes, or webpack assets via plugins
Building the HTML shell for a Gravity UI SPA
avoid: Client-side usage — this is a Node.js/server-side utility only
When streaming HTML — use generateRenderContent + renderHeadContent + renderBodyContent instead

parameters.name: plugins
parameters.description: Array of plugins that extend the rendered HTML content.

name: createDefaultPlugins
description: Creates all built-in app-layout plugins (layout, googleAnalytics, yandexMetrika, uikit, remoteVersions) as a single array for use with createRenderFunction.
when_to_use: Setting up a complete Gravity UI server render with all standard plugins in one call
avoid: When only specific plugins are needed — create individual plugins with their factory functions instead

parameters.name: options.layout
parameters.description: Options for the layout plugin (e.g. manifest path).

name: createGoogleAnalyticsPlugin
description: Creates a plugin that injects Google Analytics tracking code into the rendered HTML page.
when_to_use: Adding Google Analytics to a server-rendered Gravity UI page

name: createLayoutPlugin
description: Creates a plugin that loads scripts and stylesheets from a webpack assets manifest file and injects them into the HTML head.
when_to_use: Loading webpack-generated JS/CSS bundles into a server-rendered page automatically
signature: createLayoutPlugin(initOptions: { manifest: string; publicPath?: string }): Plugin

parameters.name: initOptions.manifest
parameters.description: Path to the webpack assets manifest JSON file.

parameters.name: initOptions.publicPath
parameters.description: Public path prefix for asset URLs.

name: createUikitPlugin
description: Creates a plugin that adds Gravity UI UIKit body attributes (theme, direction) to the server-rendered HTML body tag.
when_to_use: Applying UIKit theme and text direction to the HTML body server-side
Preventing flash of unstyled content for dark/light theme

name: createYandexMetrikaPlugin
description: Creates a plugin that injects Yandex Metrika analytics counters into the server-rendered HTML page.
when_to_use: Adding Yandex Metrika counters to a Gravity UI server-rendered application

name: createRemoteVersionsPlugin
description: Creates a plugin that exposes microfrontend version info via window.__REMOTE_VERSIONS__ for use with module federation and runtime versioning.
when_to_use: Injecting remote microfrontend version information into the HTML page for module federation
avoid: Monolith applications without module federation — this plugin is a no-op in that context

name: generateRenderContent
description: Generates the render content object (head scripts, styles, body attributes) from plugins and render params, for use in streaming HTML responses.
when_to_use: Streaming HTML responses where head content must be sent before the body is ready

name: renderBodyContent
description: Renders the HTML body content string from a RenderContent object for use in streaming responses.
when_to_use: Rendering the body portion of a streaming HTML response after async data is ready

name: renderHeadContent
description: Renders the HTML head content string (scripts, styles, meta tags) from a RenderContent object for use in streaming responses.
when_to_use: Writing the head block of a streaming HTML response before data fetching completes
